description: This lecture discusses the complexities of proof of stake (PoS) longest chain protocols, focusing on the challenges of deriving pseudorandom seeds from blockchain states and the implications of block proposer credentials. It highlights issues such as the manipulability of pseudorandom seeds and the dependence on predecessor blocks, which can incentivize nodes to exploit their choices in the blockchain.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the intricacies of PoS longest chain protocols, emphasizing the derivation of pseudorandom seeds from block proposer credentials and the potential for manipulation by nodes. It also addresses the implications of block selection on the randomness of subsequent time steps [t=0:00-0:00].
